# Create Test Plan

Generate a professional Test Plan document.

## What I Need From You
- **Project/Feature name**
- **What will be tested** (scope)
- **Test types needed** (functional, API, UI, performance, security?)
- **Team and environment info** (optional — I'll use placeholders)

## Document Sections I Produce
1. **Document Info** — project, version, author, date, status
2. **Objective** — what this test effort validates
3. **Scope** — in scope vs out of scope (explicit)
4. **Test Strategy** — test types, tools, responsible
5. **Test Environment** — URLs, databases, credentials source
6. **Entry Criteria** — what must be ready before testing starts
7. **Exit Criteria** — what must be true before testing ends
8. **Risks & Mitigations** — table with probability, impact, plan
9. **Schedule** — phases with dates and owners
10. **Deliverables** — checklist of documents to produce

## Rules
- Always include **Entry and Exit criteria** — they prevent scope creep
- Always include **Risks** — every project has them
- Use **tables** for structured data — never walls of text
- Include **placeholders** [like this] for project-specific values
- Output in **Markdown** format, ready to paste into any wiki or repo
